skill.name: ckm-ui-styling
skill.description: Build accessible React interfaces with shadcn/ui, Tailwind CSS, responsive layout patterns, theme guidance, and canvas-focused visual design references.
skill.summary: Create accessible, responsive UI systems with shadcn/ui, Tailwind CSS, and visual design guidance.
skill.author: nextlevelbuilder

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-severity patterns, but most are false positives from license text, Markdown examples, documentation URLs, and a coverage JSON artifact. No prompt injection or malicious exfiltration intent was found. The remaining real risk is medium because bundled utility scripts can run npx and write Tailwind configuration files in a target project.

medium_findings.title: Package Manager Command Execution
medium_findings.description: The shadcn helper script runs npx shadcn@latest add with user-selected component names. It uses subprocess without shell expansion, so command injection was not confirmed, but it can download packages and modify a project when executed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess calls are direct evidence of external command execution. Confidence is high for operational risk, but lower than critical because shell=True is not used and the behavior matches the skill purpose.

low_findings.title: Generated Dynamic Require Statements
low_findings.description: The Tailwind config generator emits require statements for configured plugins. The CLI path only adds recommended plugin names, but library callers should avoid passing untrusted plugin strings.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The pattern exists in generated output, not immediate execution. The default CLI uses hardcoded recommendations, so the practical risk is low unless another caller supplies untrusted plugin names.

low_findings.title: Static Findings From Documentation and Licenses Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Many reported weak cryptography, reconnaissance, hardcoded URL, and backtick findings come from SIL Open Font License text, Markdown code fences, UI examples, and documentation links. No evidence found that these examples execute during skill loading.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review confirmed representative matches are license text or fenced examples. This is a strong false-positive assessment for the repeated documentation patterns.

low_findings.title: Coverage JSON Flagged As Obfuscation
low_findings.description: The long single-line JSON file is a coverage report with executed and missing line metadata. No executable JavaScript or credential access was found in the inspected content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file begins with coverage metadata and file coverage maps. The static obfuscation signal is explained by minified JSON formatting rather than hidden code.

dangerous_patterns.title: subprocess.run Executes npx
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper script builds an argument-list command for npx shadcn@latest. This avoids shell metacharacter expansion but still executes external tooling.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command construction and subprocess.run calls are explicit. The main uncertainty is not whether execution occurs, but whether marketplace policy allows this expected helper behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Generated require Calls From Plugin Names
dangerous_patterns.description: The Tailwind generator formats plugin names into require calls in generated config files. This is useful for Tailwind plugins but should only receive trusted package names.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The require calls are generated text rather than executed by the Python script. The risk depends on how plugin names are supplied outside the default CLI flow.

content.value_statement: Teams need consistent interfaces that work across devices and assistive technologies. This skill provides shadcn/ui, Tailwind CSS, theme, responsive, and visual design guidance for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.
actual_capabilities: Guides shadcn/ui component selection, installation, composition, and common usage patterns.
Provides Tailwind CSS utility guidance for layout, spacing, typography, color, and responsive behavior.
Explains dark mode, CSS variables, theme tokens, and component customization workflows.
Includes accessibility guidance for forms, dialogs, keyboard navigation, focus states, and screen readers.
Offers Python helper scripts for adding shadcn components and generating Tailwind configuration files.
Includes canvas-oriented visual design references and bundled font assets for visual compositions.
limitations: The shadcn helper script requires Node.js, npm, and an initialized project configuration.
The skill provides guidance and examples; it does not validate final UI quality automatically.
Some examples are framework-specific and may need adaptation for non-React projects.
Utility scripts can modify project files, so users should review changes before committing them.

use_cases.title: Ship a Product Dashboard
use_cases.description: Create responsive cards, tables, dialogs, forms, and navigation with shadcn/ui and Tailwind CSS.

use_cases.title: Standardize a Design System
use_cases.description: Define shared theme tokens, dark mode behavior, component variants, and accessibility expectations.

use_cases.title: Prototype Visual Concepts
use_cases.description: Use canvas design references, font assets, and composition rules to generate polished visual directions.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Component
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a single UI element
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to build a responsive shadcn/ui card for a SaaS metrics panel. Include accessible labels and Tailwind classes.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Form Flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate workflow with validation and feedback
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design a shadcn/ui account settings form with validation states, error messages, keyboard support, and dark mode styles.

prompt_templates.title: Extend a Theme
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create a Tailwind and shadcn theme plan for a B2B analytics app with CSS variables, color tokens, and component variants.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a UI System
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review across accessibility and responsiveness
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review my React dashboard for shadcn/ui consistency, Tailwind class quality, responsive layout issues, and accessibility gaps.

output_examples.input: Design a responsive account settings screen.
output: A structured component plan using tabs, form fields, cards, and save actions.
Tailwind layout guidance for mobile, tablet, and desktop widths.
Accessibility notes for labels, errors, focus order, and keyboard navigation.

output_examples.input: Create a dark mode theme for a product dashboard.
output: A token strategy for background, foreground, border, primary, muted, and destructive colors.
Guidance for CSS variables, theme toggles, and component variant consistency.
Review notes for contrast, hover states, disabled states, and chart colors.

output_examples.input: Improve the visual quality of a landing section.
output: A composition direction with hierarchy, spacing, typography, and image placement guidance.
Recommendations for restrained color use and responsive section structure.

best_practices: Start with semantic HTML and accessible shadcn/ui primitives before adding custom styling.
Use Tailwind tokens consistently for spacing, color, typography, borders, and responsive behavior.
Run helper scripts in a version-controlled project and review generated file changes.
anti_patterns: Do not paste component examples without adapting labels, focus states, and validation behavior.
Do not pass untrusted plugin names or file paths into utility scripts.
Do not rely on visual polish alone; verify responsiveness, contrast, and keyboard behavior.

faq.question: What frameworks does this skill support?
faq.answer: It focuses on React-based projects and includes guidance for Next.js, Vite, Remix, and similar stacks.

faq.question: Does it install shadcn/ui automatically?
faq.answer: It includes a helper script for shadcn components, but users must run it intentionally in their project.

faq.question: Can it help with accessibility?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers labels, ARIA patterns, keyboard navigation, focus management, and screen reader feedback.

faq.question: Can it create Tailwind configuration files?
faq.answer: Yes. The Tailwind helper can generate configuration files with colors, fonts, spacing, breakpoints, and recommended plugins.

faq.question: Is it limited to shadcn/ui?
faq.answer: No. It also includes Tailwind utilities, responsive design, theming, accessibility, and canvas visual design references.

faq.question: What should users review before publishing changes?
faq.answer: Users should review generated components, configuration files, package changes, responsive behavior, and accessibility states.
